event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with chaotic street scenes in Beirut, featuring a building engulfed in flames and thick black smoke, observed by crowds recording the event, while a narrator questions the reliability of 'happiest or angriest countries' polls, highlighting Lebanon and Jordan in a graphic overlay. The team's plan to investigate Lebanon's 'angriest country' reputation by "going deep with the locals" without booking a hotel is detailed, emphasizing the challenge of making friends and finding accommodation within hours of arrival. Nighttime street scenes introduce Ammar, wearing a white cap, and Staffan, in a black t-shirt, walking through the city, alongside Thomas, the narrator, also in a white t-shirt. They are to be dropped in three different neighborhoods with diverse cultural and religious backgrounds, with the rule that locals "can't recognize us from YouTube." A GPS screen confirms their flight path approaching Beirut. Thomas is seen in a dimly lit alley and past a storefront, while Ammar walks along a coastal road at dusk. The segment concludes with Thomas in a well-lit room, gesturing while speaking, with a woman in a white hijab smiling beside him, as the narrator explains that "the hotel room experience doesn't always guarantee you a connection." Earlier, an upbeat track accompanies Staffan's welcome to Lebanon, with a split-screen showing Ammar laughing with an older man. The group, including Ammar and Staffan, walks through the city towards a red-facaded building, followed by an aerial shot of Beirut. Inside a bustling airport, Ammar, in a grey t-shirt and white cap, describes visiting Lebanon as a "childhood dream" and "pretty surreal." After receiving "hundreds of thousands" in cash from a fellow passenger to avoid being "ripped off," the group, including Thomas with a backpack, is warmly greeted by a man in a white t-shirt carrying a "SEEK DISCOMFORT" tote bag, who exclaims, "Let's go! It's all about that, man, this is crazy!" The mood is one of palpable camaraderie and travel excitement within the bright airport environment.

raw_segments: The video opens with intense, chaotic street scenes in Beirut, showing a building engulfed in flames and thick black smoke, with crowds of people gathered, some recording the event on their phones. A male narrator immediately sets the premise, stating, "The world's angriest country is likely not the one you think it is," and questions the reliability of polls that rank 'happiest or angriest countries.' This transitions to a graphic overlay titled 'Global Emotions report ranks top 5 angriest countries,' highlighting Lebanon and Jordan. An aerial view of a densely packed urban area, likely Beirut, accompanies the narrator's explanation of their plan: "to investigate what's really happening in this country and why it's been called the angriest." He details their challenge to "go deep with the locals" without booking a hotel, flying in and having just a few hours before dark to make friends and find a place to stay. Nighttime street scenes introduce Ammar, a man with a beard and a white cap, walking through a city street, followed by Staffan, a man with a beard and a black t-shirt, walking confidently down a cobblestone street. A split-screen formally introduces Ammar, Staffan, and Thomas (the narrator, a man with a beard and a white t-shirt), as the narrator explains, "Ammar, Staffan and myself will be dropped off in three different neighborhoods with three different cultural and religious backgrounds, hoping to get housed and get an experience with some of the locals we encounter." He adds the crucial rule: "they can't recognize us from YouTube." A GPS screen showing a flight path approaching Beirut visually confirms their journey. Thomas is then seen walking through a dimly lit alley and past a brightly lit storefront, while Ammar walks along a coastal road at dusk. The segment concludes with Thomas sitting on a bed in a well-lit room, gesturing as he speaks to someone off-camera, with a woman in a white hijab smiling beside him, as the narrator explains the "why" behind their unconventional approach, stating, "the hotel room experience doesn't always guarantee you a connection." Throughout, a consistent, slightly dramatic background music underscores the investigative narrative.

The video opens with an adventurous, upbeat background track as a male narrator (Staffan) explains their mission to 'find depth' and 'meet people,' declaring 'Holy sh*t!' before welcoming viewers to the 'Yes Theory Discovery of the country of Lebanon.' Visually, a split-screen shows Ammar, in a white cap and t-shirt, laughing at his phone alongside an older man in a light blue shirt, also laughing. The scene transitions to a car interior with a waving man, then a close-up of Staffan, now in a black t-shirt, smiling and speaking. The group, led by Ammar and Staffan, walks through a city street towards a red-facaded building, followed by a wide aerial shot of a densely packed city with 'LEBANON' overlaid. Inside a bustling airport, the group, including Ammar in a grey t-shirt and white cap, is seen laughing and interacting. Ammar is asked, 'How are you feeling, Ammar?' and responds that visiting Lebanon, a 'neighboring country' outside of Egypt, has been a 'childhood dream' and 'pretty surreal.' He is then seen counting money, with a group member noting, 'Should be enough for the taxi,' and recounting how a fellow passenger gave them 'hundreds of thousands' in cash to avoid being 'ripped off.' The narrator mentions Ammar posted about flying to Beirut. As the group, including Thomas in a white t-shirt and backpack, continues through the airport, they are warmly greeted by a man in a white t-shirt carrying a 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' tote bag. The narrator celebrates 'eight years of seeking discomfort' as the group exclaims 'Wow!' and the man with the tote bag compliments a 'nice shirt,' receiving a 'Thank you!' before enthusiastically adding, 'Let's go! It's all about that, man, this is crazy!' The cheerful and energetic mood of camaraderie and travel excitement is palpable throughout the bright, modern airport environment.

A promotional segment depicts a man in a blue suit reacting with surprise to a 'YES' sign, coinciding with advertisements for a 'Free Trip to LA' and '1st Copy of the Book,' with the audio specifically mentioning 'Matt's Yes Theory book.' The voiceover underscores the integral role of 'Seek Discomfort' in channel operations and viewer support, encouraging viewers to embrace discomfort for 'growth, connection, and adventure.' Further group photos in various locales reinforce camaraderie and brand visibility, with the audio reiterating the affordability of Seek Discomfort. The concluding scenes feature Thomas in a store and a man with a blue tote bag on a bench, illustrating the brand's integration into daily life, while ambient conversations touch upon needing items, staying with strangers, and diverse experiences, contributing to a social and adventurous atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: Inside a brightly lit space, Thomas and Staffan, among others, clap as a male voice states, "Unfortunately, you don't qualify," followed by a suggestion to "Get some money, get some some cards. And then start, get started." The scene shifts to a sun-drenched parking lot where Ammar, holding the camera, walks to and enters a white car, remarking on the heat. Driving with a woman in a striped top beside him, Ammar asks his destination, and multiple voices, including Staffan and Thomas, respond, "Hamra." In the backseat, Thomas and the man in a red tie-dye shirt look around as Ammar describes Hamra as a student area with major universities, noting students might lack housing space. He elaborates on Hamra's past fame among Arab artists and singers, its decline due to socio-economic issues and the explosion, yet its enduring beauty. Ammar explains Lebanon is rebuilding after politicians' "financial burdens," a process that will take time. The women in the backseat, one in a blue tank top and another in a striped top, smile and gesture, with one stating, "We're very positive people," and another adding, "Yeah, I'm a happy spirit and we're all like just pushing through, trying to make, you know, make every day like it's our last," eliciting a "Wow" from a male voice. The video ends with an aerial view of a cityscape with a mosque, likely Beirut, as music fades.

event_blocks.summary: A man in a white t-shirt and backpack navigates Hamra's streets, seeking the best black market currency exchange rate, with rates like "1.010 Euro" being mentioned. An Al Jazeera segment highlights Lebanon's economic crisis and the shift to a cash economy, with hands counting U.S. dollars and on-screen text detailing the collapse of the country's currency due to "a series of events and neglect." A news anchor, against a backdrop of a damaged building and a 'happy Dependence day' sign, declares Lebanon a "failed state." Dramatic footage of the 2020 Beirut blast shows a building engulfed in flames and smoke, intercut with close-ups of hands counting U.S. dollars as the narrator explains decades of political instability, corruption, mismanagement, excessive borrowing, and a lack of reforms led to a severe fiscal deficit and unsustainable debt. A graphic on Lebanon’s U.S. dollar sources notes banks halted dollar withdrawals in 2019. Scenes of a closed 'DLC Bank of Beirut' branch precede protests featuring riot police, tear gas, and crowds waving Lebanese flags, with CNN reporting on marches against corruption and tax hikes. The narrator explains that widespread protests in 2019 intensified, followed by COVID-19 and the "gigantic explosion in 2020" which caused billions in damage and sent "inflation through the roof." An aerial view of the devastated Beirut port area is shown alongside a graph illustrating Lebanese pound inflation's sharp rise from 2020 to 2023, concluding with a digital display showing $1 USD = £2,100, signifying the currency's collapse.

event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a man in a white shirt and beige pants cleaning a shoe on a concrete ledge, accompanied by upbeat travel vlog music. The scene transitions to a busy urban street where the narrator, a bearded man in a white t-shirt and backpack, explains the severe devaluation of the Lebanese Lira, stating that 1,500 Lira is now worth only 91,000, a 60-fold decrease, with another voice remarking this amount was once a family's lifetime savings. The narrator further illustrates this by noting 150 Euros, previously equivalent to $10,000, now amounts to just $150, prompting a question about how people cope. He clarifies that individuals paid in Lebanese pounds have fallen below the poverty line, a significant issue in Lebanon. An aerial view of a dense cityscape, likely Beirut, follows before returning to street level. Staffan, a bearded man in a black t-shirt and silver chain, walks and converses with the camera, expressing enthusiasm for the group and the location, calling it "the best place to start." He is joined by Ammar, in a blue-and-white t-shirt, and they walk together, laughing. A montage of thumbnails from previous 'NO MONEY' travel vlogs is shown. The group continues walking, posing for photos with arms around each other, smiling, and waving, with the narrator mentioning a plan to go to the beach, which elicits surprise and laughter. The video concludes with a hand high-fiving a man in a red tie-dye shirt as he approaches the camera amidst group laughter in a lively urban setting.

The video opens with Thomas, a man in a white t-shirt and black backpack, walking down a sunlit urban street, holding a phone and gesturing as if speaking to someone off-camera, captured from a low, dynamic perspective. It's been too many hours. Okay, so, another change of plan... These guys just told me that there's like a yes fam live gig happening in there... I'm gonna go in and say hi and check it out." A friend, a man in a black t-shirt, offers, "Well worst case scenario, I have a place you can stay over," to which Ammar emphatically replies, "But I can't do that. It has to be, it has to be a complete straight." Lively music and crowd sounds then fill the air as the group is seen in a vibrant outdoor setting with people dancing and socializing under warm lighting, where Ammar is heard exclaiming, "I was betting my life on you crashing this!" The energetic night scene transitions to a structured, promotional daytime segment, introduced by a voiceover stating, "We interrupt this program for a quick message from our sponsor." Cockney Reg, a man in a checkered shirt and turban, speaks from inside a car with a distinct British accent, promoting 'Seekers Day,' highlighting "30% off," "best sellers," "four exclusive bonuses," a "free chapter of Matt's book," and "exclusive access to one hour live Q&A with Thomas and Ammar," concluding with a "free mystery gift." Visually, three men, including one in a white long-sleeve 'Seek Discomfort' shirt, are shown walking and later conversing in a courtyard during daylight. The video ends with Cockney Reg in his car, accompanied by a graphic of a 'Mystery Gift' and a shirtless man with green hair, reinforcing the promotional and comedic tone.

event_blocks.summary: On a lively urban street at night, a man in a white t-shirt (Man in white t-shirt (street) 2) animatedly asks, 'You wanna go techno?' to which another young man (Man in white t-shirt (street) 3) agrees, while they humorously discuss police wanted status. The narrator, a man with bleached hair in a black t-shirt (Man in black t-shirt (street)), walks with a backpack and silver chain, narrating his quest to secure a place for the night, questioning if he's 'getting kidnapped' or having 'the best night ever' and asking passersby, 'Do you think I could stay at your place?' receiving responses like 'It's a bit hard' and 'It's a bit far.' Inside a café at 10:15 PM, Staffan, in a blue-and-white t-shirt and cap, contextualizes the narrator's request by comparing it to a Swedish person asking to stay at a Lebanese home. Back on the street at 10:43 PM, Staffan smiles and talks outside, followed by two men, one in a black t-shirt with a food bag and a bald man in a grey t-shirt (Bald man in grey t-shirt) who jokes, 'Wife is saying no' to the accommodation query. Later, at 11:08 PM, the man in the black t-shirt with 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' asks a Lebanese passerby about hosting him, then exclaims upon spotting someone, 'Bro, this is my territory.' He discusses making 'at least 300 friends' and the 'game of numbers' in finding accommodation, with one man holding a '0.1%' sign labeled 'EDITOR' prompting laughter. Voices inquire about good energy and the necessity of finding a place to avoid staying on the street. At 11:27 PM, the group walks past storefronts, with the narrator describing the street as a 'busy party street.' Ammar, identified as the man with bleached hair, beard, and 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' t-shirt, describes a damaged gas station near an explosion site, reflecting that Lebanon represents 'the will to live.' He encounters Kennedy, a woman in a grey t-shirt, who highlights the 'sense of community' and the feeling of 'never alone' as her favorite aspects of living in Lebanon. At 12:43 AM, Ammar approaches a woman in a green sleeveless t-shirt and a man in a black t-shirt with a watch, asking if they are Lebanese and presenting his challenge: 'to find someone who would be very spontaneous to say yes to host me tonight.' The woman checks with her flatmate, confirming it's 'feasible.' The video continues with a woman in a green sleeveless t-shirt with 'SEXY TRIPLES LITTLE HIA' and a man in a black t-shirt gesturing with a phone, as a voice states they are '5 minutes away' and praises those 'opening doors.' Amidst streetlights, an 'Ehidiac' sign, and a chalkboard menu, the vlogger introduces 'Danny' and 'Stefan,' with banter about a 'new roommate' and plans for breakfast. Exiting a van at 1:04 AM, the vlogger, in a white cap and black t-shirt with 'Love Over Fear,' asks about hosting, is told to 'find the right person,' and that they are 'traveling tomorrow.' Two young men in green and white t-shirts, holding ice cream, interact with the camera, and one asks to be in the vlog. The vlogger explains his challenge: 'investigating the angriest country in the world' by having 'each of us to find a Lebanese person to host us for the first night.' The scene shifts to two young men with ice cream, and Ammar, in a black 'Love Over Fear' t-shirt and white cap, asks, 'Do you guys have a floor that I can crash on tonight?' The young man in the white t-shirt offers to ask a friend. After a 10-minute time-lapse, Ammar excitedly announces, 'My friends told me, they found a place in Kempinski... and it's Lebanese people,' suggesting they 'Let's go, let's go meet them.'

The video opens with a man in a black 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' t-shirt, silver chain, and backpack, walking through a nighttime city street. Oh my god, bro. What the fuck is my bike? Am I scared? Yes. Um..." before a "Yeah!" and upbeat music begins. He describes the "life-loving people" of Lebanon, mentioning girls singing from car roofs, and discusses how despite the "economic crisis" and other challenges, "Fun is not an option. Fun is everywhere. It's a lifestyle. If we work, we have fun. If we study, we have fun. The Lebanese vibe is different from the rest of the world." The driver, in a white t-shirt, gestures while speaking, and a man in a red tie-dye shirt is visible in the backseat. A voice then asks, "Oh wow, this is your place?" Over the continuing upbeat music, voices exchange greetings and lighthearted comments like "Come, bro, Thomas," "Great," "No worries," "Salam," "Nice to meet you," and "It's your bed?" followed by laughter, emphasizing the casual and jovial atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: Ammar, wearing a 'Love Over Fear' t-shirt and white cap, initially exclaims "Bro, you're freaking crazy, man" in a dimly lit space before narrating emotionally from a moving vehicle at night about a friend's anger and being denied access to a chalet, with the Beirut explosion's destruction visible and a '8.8.2020' banner in the background. As upbeat music starts, Ammar declares "All right, let's go!" and walks through a narrow alley with two young men, one in a yellow shirt and another in a white t-shirt, stating, "You never get an experience like this staying in a hotel," amidst a car horn sound. He greets someone with "What's up, bro?" and a young man replies, "Nice to meet you," while Ammar inquires, "You know everyone in this neighborhood, huh?" to which a young man confirms, "Yeah," and another adds, "He knows everyone." A scooter passes, and then Staffan, in a blue-and-white t-shirt and cap, enters a dimly lit apartment. Ammar announces, "We're here," as Staffan enters a modern living room with a TV. Ammar offers Pringles and nuts to Staffan and a man in a white t-shirt with a tree graphic, who laughs and gestures, while another male voice requests nuts. Staffan sits, takes Pringles, and smiles, as Ammar mentions ice cream, chips, and watching a movie, but another male voice corrects him, "No, we're having a sleepover," to which Ammar responds, "No." The scene shifts to a hotel room where the man in the white t-shirt with a tree graphic is seated, discussing "no pillow fights." In the hallway, a man in a black t-shirt with 'QINX' fails to open door '1038' with a key card, joined by another man in a white t-shirt and red shorts. Ammar explains that he lost his accommodation due to interpersonal drama, with on-screen text confirming, 'He already cancelled your key,' and the group jokes about sleeping on the beach before exiting. Outdoors at night, Thomas, identified by dialogue and wearing a black t-shirt with 'WAX', discusses a club called "UFO" before entering a narrow alley with others, including a man in a black t-shirt with 'THOMAS ONLY' and the man in the 'QINX' shirt. Inside the alley, lively conversation ensues with laughter and gestures, featuring dialogue like "I think I've met the mayor here," and "You know everyone, bro," culminating in an exclamation of "Thomas!"

The scene transitions to a panoramic cityscape mural overlaid with 'The same day' text, as the man reappears, now shirtless and sitting up in bed, smiling and gesturing, his voice becoming more energetic as he announces, "Good morning and welcome to day two of the Lebanon adventure," and then, "Okay, I just woke up." The setting shifts to a warmly lit indoor space where the man, now in an orange t-shirt and white cap, speaks to the camera with a smile, gesturing expressively, interacting with someone off-camera with "You're awake?" which is met with a female "Yes," followed by his exclamations of "Wow!" and "Oh my God!". The video then moves outdoors to a scenic mountainous valley where the same man, now in a white t-shirt, takes a selfie with two women—one in a white crop top and black pants, the other in a black top and jeans—walking behind him, all smiling. The man's energetic voice, joined by the women's voices, loudly declares, "Welcome to Lebanon!" The scene changes again to a sunny beach, where the man, now in a black t-shirt, holds a towel and speaks to the camera with the ocean and palm trees in the background, stating, "I think we're going to the beach." The final shot shows a group, including a man in a white patterned shirt and another in a yellow-and-black cap, walking toward the beach under a bright blue sky, carrying bags, as the main man playfully remarks, "I'm the only foreigner here." Upbeat, Middle Eastern-style music plays throughout, setting an adventurous mood.

event_blocks.summary: A man swims in the ocean, followed by scenes of a crowded beach where a man in a red life vest declares "We're in heaven" and "Beautiful day," agreeing with a companion in a grey tank top. The group then dines in a rustic restaurant with a sea view, enjoying bread and olives, with one man announcing "Hello, breakfast time." The man in the red life vest, now wearing sunglasses, rides a jet ski with a city skyline in the background, later joined by a passenger, both exclaiming "Let's go!" and "Woo!" as they navigate rocky coastal areas, with the man stating, "As I told you, it's a captain's day." After the jet ski speeds through a narrow rock passage, a man in a blue t-shirt walks through a stone archway in a historic building, exclaiming "Welcome to Lebanon!" A reflective tune plays as a woman in a blue tank top walks through a narrow alley and a man in a white t-shirt converses, before a wide cityscape from a high vantage point is shown. The narrative continues with a man in a white t-shirt and striped pants walking through an ornate interior, who later, wearing a 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' t-shirt, narrates spending days meeting locals and exploring Beirut. A brightly lit dining room scene shows a group, including the man in the 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' t-shirt and a woman in a white blouse with a blue hijab, enjoying a meal, with exclamations of "This is really good." The man states, "I think Lebanon and Syria have the best food in the world," affirmed by the woman. She is seen in a kitchen with red tiled walls, describing Lebanese people as "the happiest, desperate people" and recounting a personal experience during the revolution where people were "drinking beer, playing cards" amidst unrest. This communal meal is briefly contrasted with a tense outdoor protest scene featuring Lebanese flags and tear gas before returning to the group's conversation. The narrator concludes by explaining the challenge of staying with strangers and immersing further into the culture of the country.

raw_segments: The video opens with a man swimming in the ocean, his face close to the camera amidst splashing water under a bright blue sky, accompanied by upbeat, rhythmic music and an excited "Woo!" The scene quickly shifts to a crowded beach with people playing and lounging, as another excited voice exclaims "Wow!" Two men then smile at the camera on the sunny beach; the man in a red life vest and white cap declares "We're in heaven," "Beautiful day," and "We're in paradise," while the man in a grey tank top agrees, "Beautiful day, yes." The setting transitions to a group dining in a rustic, stone-walled restaurant with a sea view, enjoying bread, olives, and drinks. A voice announces "Hello, breakfast time," another states, "We just met, met on the street," followed by an excited "Yeah, baby!" as a man in a black t-shirt eats and a woman in a green sleeveless top gestures. The action then cuts to the man in the red life vest, now wearing sunglasses, riding a jet ski with a city skyline in the background, with a voice noting, "Got this guy joining our squad today." He is later seen with a passenger, both laughing and shouting "Let's go!" and "Woo!" as they navigate rocky coastal areas, repeatedly exclaiming "Oh my god!" and "Wow!" The man in the red life vest confidently states, "As I told you, it's a captain's day, you know, right here." After a panoramic city view and the jet ski speeding through a narrow rock passage with engine sounds, a man in a blue t-shirt, along with others, walks through a stone archway in a historic building, exclaiming "Welcome to Lebanon!" The upbeat music fades, replaced by a more reflective tune as a woman in a blue tank top walks through a narrow alley and a man in a white t-shirt converses. The video concludes with a wide cityscape from a high vantage point, as a male narrator explains, "After completing our original challenge of staying with strangers and experiencing something with them, in an effort to dive deeper and immerse ourselves further into the culture of this beautiful country..."

The segment opens with a wide shot of a grand, ornate interior, possibly a historic building or mosque, featuring arched doorways and intricate tilework, as a man in a white t-shirt and striped pants walks through. A gentle, traditional string and percussion melody plays in the background. This man, later seen wearing a 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' t-shirt, narrates, "We spent the next few days meeting locals, exploring Beirut, and of course, trying some authentic homemade Lebanese food." The scene quickly transitions to a modern, brightly lit dining room where a group of people, including the man in the 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' t-shirt and a woman in a white blouse with a blue hijab, are gathered around a table laden with food. Laughter and lively chatter fill the air, with one male voice exclaiming, "This is really good," and another adding, "This is crazy good." Close-ups show hands serving food, including a dish with chickpeas and a salad with dried tomatoes. The man in the 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' shirt shares, "I've been fortunate to travel to many places, but I think Lebanon and Syria have the best food in the world," to which the woman in the hijab affirms, "They do." She is seen preparing a dish, pouring a white liquid into a glass baking dish, and later in a kitchen with red tiled walls. Amidst the warm, communal dining, the woman in the hijab describes the Lebanese people as "the happiest, desperate people," explaining how "one day you hear on the news there's clash in the streets, people are fighting, and the next day they are partying." She recounts a personal experience during the revolution, saying, "one day I was on the street and Hadi called me and told me, 'Mom, come pick me up.' People were on the streets drinking beer, playing cards," concluding with a reflective, "And I was like, where do we live?" The man in the 'SEEK DISCOMFORT' shirt interjects with a "Yeah." This communal meal is briefly contrasted with a tense outdoor protest scene featuring people waving Lebanese flags and tear gas in the air, before returning to the group's animated conversation and meal.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with hands spreading a white, creamy substance onto a plate, set against gentle, melodic background music. The scene transitions to a modern living room where a group of men in white t-shirts interact jovially around a coffee table filled with gift boxes, sampling food and exclaiming delight. A voice welcomes them, 'Nice to meet you.' Inside a moving vehicle, a man in a white cap drives while voices react to a growing crowd, noting 'There's a mob out there' and confirming a 'meetup' related to the 'Yes Fam community' in Lebanon, described as 'super active' with a 'strong support system.' As sunset approaches, the men in white t-shirts walk along a coastal road, their silhouettes backlit by golden light. They pause on a concrete ledge overlooking the sea, where one man holds a Lebanese flag, another records with a camera, and a third gestures. A large crowd of onlookers gathers, recording and cheering, erupting into chants of 'Lebanon! Yes Fam! Yes Fam!'. The man with the flag thanks the crowd for their community involvement and support, calling them 'some of the kindest, most hospitable, most life-loving people.' He proudly states that 'Lebanon was one of the first communities to get activated' and that 'Yes Fam rising in the Middle East before any other country,' as the crowd's 'Yes Fam! Yes Fam!' chants intensify against the city skyline and sea.

event_blocks.summary: As dusk settles, a group of young men, including a man in a red tie-dye shirt and glasses holding a camera and another in a white t-shirt with a tree graphic, observe a coastal road and city skyline from a concrete ledge. The scene shifts to a woman in a white crop top and maroon skirt gesturing to a large, diverse crowd on a grassy hill, many holding phones. A woman's voice recounts positive experiences of marriage and engagement within their group, prompting the crowd to chant "YES! YES! YES!". A man's voice extols the "incredible" sense of community in Lebanon, emphasizing the constant presence of family and friends, and his personal discovery of community. He announces the celebration of "eight years of Yes Theory and Seekers Day" and proposes getting a "Yes tattoo". Close-ups capture excited reactions from individuals in the crowd, such as a man in a grey shirt and a man in a white t-shirt with 'SEEK DISCOMFORT'. The crowd's cheers escalate as a man asks, "Is that a yes?", met with a roaring "It's a yes!". Inspiring music begins as the scene transitions to a man in a black t-shirt lying on a bed while another man in a black t-shirt and gloves tattoos his leg, while a voiceover reflects on people finding strength and courage during challenging times. The video concludes with a group of men in white t-shirts, including one with a cap and silver chain, laughing on the seaside ledge near a Lebanese flag, followed by a brief glimpse of a flower shop interior where a man in a blue shirt, a woman in a patterned blouse arrange flowers, and a man in a blue-and-white t-shirt smiles.
